skill.name: gene-info
skill.description: Retrieves comprehensive gene information including PubMed publication counts, NCBI summaries, and Ensembl transcript data. Supports batch processing and file input. Invoke when the user asks for gene details, publication statistics, or needs to analyze a list of genes.
skill.summary: Queries NCBI Gene, PubMed, and Ensembl to return publication counts, gene summaries, and transcript data for one or more genes.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 108 potential issues with a risk score of 100/100, but AI evaluation confirms nearly all are FALSE POSITIVES. The high-severity findings for 'API keys', 'certificate files', and 'weak cryptography' are all pattern-matching errors: Python dict .keys() was mistaken for certificate files, and words like 'description' and 'get' were flagged as cryptographic algorithm names. The remaining LOW findings confirm expected behavior: HTTP requests to hardcoded URLs for NCBI and Ensembl (public scientific databases), optional API key usage for rate limiting, and file writing to user-specified output paths. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or malicious intent was detected. This is a legitimate bioinformatics research tool with minimal risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to Public Scientific APIs
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs for NCBI E-utilities (eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov) and Ensembl REST API (rest.ensembl.org). These are legitimate public scientific databases. No data exfiltration risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading source code - URLs point to public NCBI and Ensembl API endpoints, no suspicious destinations.

low_findings.title: Optional API Key from Environment Variables
low_findings.description: The skill reads NCBI_API_KEY and NCBI_EMAIL from environment variables. These are optional credentials for accessing public NCBI databases with higher rate limits. No credentials are transmitted to third parties.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by source code review - API key is only passed to NCBI E-utilities for rate limiting, never sent elsewhere.

low_findings.title: File Write to User-Specified Output Path
low_findings.description: The skill writes results to a user-specified output file via --output flag. This supports JSON and CSV export. The output path is set by the user at invocation time, presenting minimal risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by source code review - file writing is part of the data export feature, path is user-specified via CLI argument.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Execution Described in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents bash commands for running the Python script (e.g., python scripts/fetch_gene_info.py). These are usage examples expected for a CLI-based skill. No dynamic command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All shell commands in SKILL.md are static bash usage examples in code blocks, not dynamic execution. Expected for a CLI tool.

content.user_title: Retrieve Gene Information from PubMed, NCBI, and Ensembl
content.value_statement: Searching for gene information across multiple databases is time-consuming. This skill automates the process by fetching publication counts, NCBI summaries, and Ensembl transcript data in one step.
actual_capabilities: Retrieves PubMed publication counts for specified gene symbols
Fetches official gene summaries and descriptions from NCBI Gene database
Queries Ensembl for transcript counts and maximum amino acid lengths
Processes multiple genes in batch with parallel execution
Reads gene lists from file input (CSV, text, TSV formats)
Exports results to JSON or CSV format
limitations: Requires internet access to query external NCBI and Ensembl databases
Limited to genes indexed in NCBI and Ensembl reference databases
PubMed publication counts depend on NCBI database linkage accuracy
Processing speed depends on NCBI API rate limits and available bandwidth

use_cases.title: Literature Review Preparation
use_cases.description: Quickly gather publication statistics for a set of genes to assess research coverage before writing a literature review.

use_cases.title: Gene Panel Validation
use_cases.description: Validate gene symbols and retrieve structural information such as transcript counts and chromosome locations for a gene panel.

use_cases.title: Grant Application Support
use_cases.description: Collect comprehensive gene summaries and ortholog data to support preliminary data sections in grant proposals.

prompt_templates.title: Single Gene Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the gene-info skill on the gene BRCA1.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs quick information about a single gene.

prompt_templates.title: Gene Publications with Keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the gene-info skill on the gene TP53. Filter PubMed results with keyword 'lung cancer'.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs publication counts filtered by a disease keyword.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Gene Analysis with Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the gene-info skill on the genes BRCA1, TP53, and EGFR. Include homology data and export results to genes_report.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to analyze multiple genes with homology data and file output.

prompt_templates.title: File-Based Gene Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the gene-info skill using the file genes.txt. Process all genes in the list and save the output to results.csv.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a prepared list of genes in a file for batch processing.

output_examples.output: The skill returned TP53 details: found on chromosome 17 in Homo sapiens. NCBI description: 'tumor protein p53'. Ensembl has 15 transcripts with a max of 393 amino acids. Total PubMed publications: 128,542.

The skill processed all 3 genes. BRCA1 (chr17): 98,430 publications, 52 transcripts. BRCA2 (chr13): 27,841 publications, 27 transcripts. TP53 (chr17): 128,542 publications, 15 transcripts. Results were exported to JSON.

output_examples.input: Get gene information with keyword 'breast cancer'
output_examples.output: Gene BRCA1 has 98,430 total publications and 32,150 with keyword 'breast cancer'. Gene structure from Ensembl shows 52 transcripts and max 1,863 amino acids. NCBI summary: 'E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase that plays a key role in DNA repair.'

best_practices: Provide your NCBI email address using the NCBI_EMAIL environment variable to follow NCBI usage guidelines and get better support.
Use batch processing with multiple genes to reduce total runtime compared to running one gene at a time.
Start with a small test set of genes to verify the output format before processing large lists.
anti_patterns: Running the skill without a stable internet connection will result in failed database queries and incomplete results.
Processing genes with symbols that contain special characters or whitespace may cause lookup failures in external databases.
Requesting genomic sequences for every gene in a large batch without need wastes API quota and increases processing time.

faq.question: What databases does this skill query?
faq.answer: It queries NCBI Gene, NCBI PubMed, and Ensembl databases.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, an API key is optional. It provides higher rate limits from NCBI.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for input?
faq.answer: The skill supports plain text, CSV, TSV, and space or comma-separated lists.

faq.question: How many genes can I process at once?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit. Processing time depends on the number of genes and API rate limits.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access?
faq.answer: Yes, it requires internet access to query NCBI and Ensembl databases.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Results can be printed to the console or saved as JSON or CSV files.
